content: Working memory capacity limits on agent reasoning can be resolved by treating external tool-accessed memory as a dynamically expandable "curiosity-curated scratchpad", where curiosity-driven reinforcement learning selectively offloads only high-uncertainty, high-reward information to tool-based memory instead of dumping all contextual data. This creates a self-tuning tradeoff that avoids both overloading the agent's native working memory and causing costly tool retrieval latency.
source_topics: agent working memory capacity limits reasoning
trigger_topic: agent working memory capacity limits reasoning
